title: Arjuna’s Moral Dilemma
summary: Arjuna hesitates to fight his own relatives in war.
ethical_principle: Duty must be guided by righteousness, not emotion alone.

title: Yudhishthira and Truth
summary: Yudhishthira struggles between truth and strategic deception.
ethical_principle: Truth is supreme but complex in crisis situations.

title: Karna’s Loyalty
summary: Karna remains loyal to Duryodhana despite knowing the truth.
ethical_principle: Loyalty should not override justice.

title: Bhishma’s Vow
summary: Bhishma sacrifices personal happiness for a vow.
ethical_principle: Extreme vows may lead to unintended consequences.

title: Draupadi’s Question
summary: Draupadi questions the legality of her humiliation.
ethical_principle: Silence against injustice enables wrongdoing.

title: Vidura’s Wisdom
summary: Vidura gives ethical advice ignored by rulers.
ethical_principle: Wise counsel should be valued over ego.

title: Abhimanyu’s Bravery
summary: Abhimanyu enters battle despite incomplete knowledge.
ethical_principle: Courage must be supported by preparation.

title: Krishna’s Diplomacy
summary: Krishna attempts peace before war.
ethical_principle: Peaceful negotiation should precede conflict.

title: Ekalavya’s Sacrifice
summary: Ekalavya gives his thumb as guru dakshina.
ethical_principle: Blind obedience can be unjust.

title: Duryodhana’s Greed
summary: Duryodhana refuses to share power.
ethical_principle: Greed destroys relationships and nations.

title: Gandhari’s Blindfold
summary: Gandhari blinds herself in loyalty.
ethical_principle: Misplaced loyalty leads to moral blindness.

title: The Dice Game
summary: Yudhishthira gambles away everything.
ethical_principle: Addiction and poor judgment lead to downfall.

title: Kunti’s Secret
summary: Kunti hides Karna’s true identity.
ethical_principle: Hidden truths can create long-term conflict.

title: Ashwatthama’s Revenge
summary: Ashwatthama seeks revenge after his father’s death.
ethical_principle: Revenge perpetuates suffering.

title: Bhima’s Oath
summary: Bhima vows revenge for Draupadi.
ethical_principle: Justice must not become uncontrolled vengeance.

title: Nakula and Sahadeva’s Humility
summary: The twins remain humble despite royal status.
ethical_principle: Humility strengthens leadership.

title: Shikhandi’s Role
summary: Shikhandi plays key role in Bhishma’s fall.
ethical_principle: Strategy can change power dynamics.

title: Krishna and Karma
summary: Krishna teaches action without attachment.
ethical_principle: Right action matters more than reward.

title: The Yaksha Prashna
summary: Yudhishthira answers moral questions wisely.
ethical_principle: Wisdom arises from reflection.

title: End of War Reflection
summary: After victory, the cost of war becomes clear.
ethical_principle: Even justified conflict carries heavy cost.
